$comment: This schema is normatively defined in docs/aip-spec.md Section 4.2. Do not modify this file without updating the RFC document accordingly.
title: AIP Principal Token
description: A signed JWT payload encoding one delegation link in the AIP principal chain. Defined in AIP-CORE-v0.1 §4.3. These tokens are embedded as compact-serialised JWTs in the aip_chain array of a Credential Token — they are not transmitted as standalone objects. Each element in aip_chain is a complete JWT (header.payload.signature) whose payload conforms to this schema.

sub.description: AID of the agent this token authorises. The agent identified here is the one being granted the scopes listed in this token.

principal.description: The root principal — the human or organisation ultimately responsible for this agent's actions. This object is carried unchanged through every link in the delegation chain.

type.description: Whether the root principal is a human individual or an organisational entity.

id.description: W3C DID of the ROOT principal — the human or organisation at the top of the entire delegation chain. CRITICAL: This field MUST be byte-for-byte identical across every Principal Token in the same aip_chain array. It always identifies the originating human or organisation — never an intermediate delegating agent. Relying parties MUST reject chains where this value differs between any two elements. MUST NOT use the did:aip method (root principals are humans or organisations, never AIP agents).

oneOf.description: AID of the parent agent that issued this delegation. REQUIRED when delegation_depth > 0. Validation Algorithm Step 8e verifies that this value equals the sub field of the previous chain element (aip_chain[i-1].sub).
MUST be null when delegation_depth is 0 — this token was issued directly by the root principal, with no intermediate agent.

delegated_by.description: AID of the parent agent that issued this delegation. Null when the root principal issued directly. The chain linkage rule: delegated_by in token at index i MUST equal sub in token at index i-1.

delegation_depth.description: Position of this token in the delegation chain. MUST equal the array index of this token in aip_chain (0 for root, 1 for first sub-delegation, etc.). Enforced by Validation Algorithm Rule D-4. Hard maximum 10.

max_delegation_depth.description: Maximum total delegation depth permitted from this chain's root. Set by the root principal in aip_chain[0] and MUST NOT be increased by any agent in the chain (Rule D-2). A value of 0 means the authorised agent is a leaf — it cannot spawn sub-agents. Default when absent: 3. Hard maximum: 10. Only the value from aip_chain[0] governs the entire chain — values in subsequent tokens are informational only.

issued_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime string when this delegation was issued (e.g., 2026-03-22T10:00:00Z). Note: Principal Token timestamps are ISO 8601 strings; Credential Token timestamps (iat/exp) are Unix integers. When comparing during chain validation, parse ISO 8601 to Unix timestamp. Clock skew tolerance: 30 seconds.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime string after which this delegation is invalid. MUST be strictly after issued_at — zero-duration and back-dated delegations are rejected by Validation Algorithm Step 8h with chain_token_expired.

purpose.description: OPTIONAL. Human-readable description of this agent's intended purpose. Not machine-enforced — informational only for audit and transparency.

oneOf.description: Unique identifier for the specific task this delegation authorises.
Null when this delegation is not associated with a specific task.

task_id.description: OPTIONAL for personal, enterprise, service, orchestrator agents. REQUIRED (non-null, non-empty) for ephemeral agents. Links this delegation to a specific task in the audit trail. Validated during registration (check 11 in §11.5).

items.description: A scope string in dot-notation format. Defined values listed in AIP-CORE-v0.1 §5.2.

scope.description: The capability scopes being delegated by this token. MUST be a subset of the delegating party's own Capability Manifest scopes (scope inheritance rule, §5.4). Duplicate entries are prohibited.

then.description: When delegation_depth >= 1, delegated_by MUST be a non-null did:aip AID.

else.description: When delegation_depth == 0, delegated_by MUST be null (issued directly by the root principal).

$comment: Principal Tokens are JWTs — they use standard JWT header signing, not a custom canonicalization order. The JWT signing covers the compact base64url(header).base64url(payload) string. See AIP-CORE-v0.1 §4.3 and §4.4 for delegation rules D-1 through D-5.
